AISuggestion.name: Test5
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine returns to WaitingForFirstCoin immediately after CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered, but the specification states that the machine becomes ready for a new order only once the beverage has been collected. The model therefore misses the collection step between drink completion and readiness.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add an explicit post-preparation state and event for beverage collection in CoffeeMachine. After coffee or tea preparation completes, remain in a delivered or ready-to-collect state until a collection action occurs, and only then transition back to WaitingForFirstCoin.

AISuggestion.name: Test4
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine behavior does not match the specification for drink preparation time: after a tea or coffee selection, the model immediately reaches delivery after `beverageReady` and the timing attributes allow ranges (`teaMinDelay=3`, `teaMaxDelay=8`, `coffeeMinDelay=5`, `coffeeMaxDelay=10`) instead of the required fixed delays of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Update CoffeeMachine so that selecting tea triggers a fixed 8-second preparation before TeaDelivered, and selecting coffee triggers a fixed 10-second preparation before CoffeeDelivered. Remove the ranged timing parameters or replace them with exact preparation delays used by the state machine transitions.

AISuggestion.name: OverspecifiedDesign_Test3
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine model does not represent the specified beverage preparation behavior correctly: after tea or coffee selection, it immediately reaches TeaDelivered or CoffeeDelivered without modeling the required fixed preparation durations of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee before the beverage is available.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that pressing the tea button leads to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and pressing the coffee button leads to a coffee preparation state with a 10-second delay, before reaching the delivered state.

AISuggestion.name: OverspecifiedDesign_Test2
AISuggestion.description: The model contains a CappuccinoButton and related cappuccino interaction in the coffee machine, but the specification defines only two beverage buttons: tea and coffee. This introduces an unsupported feature that is not part of the system specification.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Remove the CappuccinoButton block, its signal types and connections, and any cappuccino-related ports or receptions from the CoffeeMachine so that the model exposes only tea and coffee selections.

AISuggestion.name: OverspecifiedDesign_Test1
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine model delivers coffee and tea immediately after button selection, but the specification requires the machine to spend 10 seconds preparing a coffee and 8 seconds preparing a tea before the beverage is delivered.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after receiving pushCoffeeButton or pushTeaButton, it enters distinct preparation states with timed transitions of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before reaching the delivered states.

TURTLEGMODELING.SPECIFICATION:
This coffee machine dispenses a beverage only after two coins have been deposited. If there's a substantial delay between (i.e., more than 30 seconds) the insertion of the first and second coins, the machine returns the initial coin. Likewise, if a beverage isn't selected promptly after the deposit of the two coins, both coins are automatically ejected. If either of the beverage buttons (tea or coffee) is pressed before the coins are ejected, the machine begins to prepare the selected drink. Notably, it takes 10 seconds to brew a coffee and 8 seconds to make a tea. Once the beverage has been collected, the machine is ready to accept new coins for the next order.
The user is represented as a Wallet that contains two coins when the system starts. The wallet takes between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
Use between 3 and 6 blocks.

textline.data: The availability of the machine shall be
A wallet must never contain a
The coffee machine never has one coin
inserted more than 50 units of time
When the application starts, if the wallet contains
at least 2 coins, then a beverage may be delivered.
On the contrary, if the wallet contains less than 2
coins, no beverage will be delivered by the

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value: CoffeeMachine featuring two main functions: - coin ejection - tea or coffee selection

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value:
A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2
A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1
F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin
CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Suggestion* Computation time: 319 s Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Model improvement: 0
Description: The CoffeeMachine model delivers coffee and tea immediately after button selection, but the specification requires the machine to spend 10 seconds preparing a coffee and 8 seconds preparing a tea before the beverage is delivered.
How to solve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after receiving pushCoffeeButton or pushTeaButton, it enters distinct preparation states with timed transitions of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before reaching the delivered states.

Line.value: Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
The CoffeeMachine model delivers coffee and tea immediately
after button selection, but the specification requires the machine to spend 10 seconds preparing a coffee and 8 seconds preparing a tea before the beverage is delivered.
Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after receiving pushCoffeeButton or pushTeaButton, it enters distinct preparation states with timed transitions of
10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before reaching the delivered states.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value: CoffeeMachine featuring two main functions: - coin ejection - tea or coffee selection

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value:
A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2
A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1
F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin
CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 134 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 10 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification
Mutation: remove connection from push in CappuccinoButton to pushCappuccinoButton in CoffeeMachine remove signal pushCappuccinoButton in CoffeeMachine remove block CappuccinoButton
Description: The model contains a CappuccinoButton and related cappuccino interaction in the coffee machine, but the specification defines only two beverage buttons: tea and coffee. This introduces an unsupported feature that is not part of the system specification.
How to solve: Remove the CappuccinoButton block, its signal types and connections, and any cappuccino-related ports or receptions from the CoffeeMachine so that the model exposes only tea and coffee selections.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification
remove connection from push in CappuccinoButton to pushCappuccinoButton in CoffeeMachine
The model contains a CappuccinoButton and related cappuccino interaction in the coffee machine,
but the specification defines only two beverage buttons: tea and coffee. This introduces an unsupported feature that is not part of the system specification.
Remove the CappuccinoButton block, its signal types and connections, and any cappuccino-related ports or receptions from the CoffeeMachine so that the
model exposes only tea and coffee selections.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

infoparam.value:
*Suggestion* Computation time: 273 s Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Model improvement: 0
Description: The CoffeeMachine model does not represent the specified beverage preparation behavior correctly: after tea or coffee selection, it immediately reaches TeaDelivered or CoffeeDelivered without modeling the required fixed preparation durations of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee before the beverage is available.
How to solve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that pressing the tea button leads to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and pressing the coffee button leads to a coffee preparation state with a 10-second delay, before reaching the delivered state.

Line.value: Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
The CoffeeMachine model does not represent the specified beverage preparation behavior correctly: after tea or coffee selection,
it immediately reaches TeaDelivered or CoffeeDelivered without modeling the required fixed preparation durations of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee before the beverage is available.
Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that pressing the tea button leads to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and pressing the coffee button leads to a
coffee preparation state with a 10-second delay, before reaching the delivered state.

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Suggestion* Computation time: 269 s Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Model improvement: 0
Description: The CoffeeMachine behavior does not match the specification for drink preparation time: after a tea or coffee selection, the model immediately reaches delivery after `beverageReady` and the timing attributes allow ranges (`teaMinDelay=3`, `teaMaxDelay=8`, `coffeeMinDelay=5`, `coffeeMaxDelay=10`) instead of the required fixed delays of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee.
How to solve: Update CoffeeMachine so that selecting tea triggers a fixed 8-second preparation before TeaDelivered, and selecting coffee triggers a fixed 10-second preparation before CoffeeDelivered. Remove the ranged timing parameters or replace them with exact preparation delays used by the state machine transitions.

Line.value: Semantic oracle evaluation of SysML v2 fallback: 10
The CoffeeMachine behavior does not match the specification for drink preparation time: after a tea or
coffee selection, the model immediately reaches delivery after `beverageReady` and the timing attributes
required fixed delays of 8 seconds for tea and 10 seconds for coffee.
Update CoffeeMachine so that selecting tea triggers a fixed 8-second preparation before TeaDelivered, and
selecting coffee triggers a fixed 10-second preparation before CoffeeDelivered. Remove the ranged timing parameters
or replace them with exact preparation delays used by the state machine transitions.

infoparam.value: CoffeeMachine featuring two main functions: - coin ejection - tea or coffee selection

A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2
A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1
F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin
CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 277 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 6
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 7.5 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 5 Model improvement: 0 Comments: the suggestion is incomplete (it does not specifiy to add another block to produce the newly introduced event The script only partially implements it. As a result, the CoffeeMachine's SMD features an important regression.
Mutation: add input signal beverageCollected in CoffeeMachine remove transition in CoffeeMachine from CoffeeDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin remove transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
Description: The CoffeeMachine returns to WaitingForFirstCoin immediately after CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered, but the specification states that the machine becomes ready for a new order only once the beverage has been collected. The model therefore misses the collection step between drink completion and readiness.
How to solve: Add an explicit post-preparation state and event for beverage collection in CoffeeMachine. After coffee or tea preparation completes, remain in a delivered or ready-to-collect state until a collection action occurs, and only then transition back to WaitingForFirstCoin.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 5
Comments: the suggestion is incomplete (it does not specifiy to add another block to produce the newly introduced event
The script only partially implements it. As a result, the CoffeeMachine's SMD features an important regression.
add input signal beverageCollected in CoffeeMachine
remove transition in CoffeeMachine from CoffeeDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
remove transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
The CoffeeMachine returns to WaitingForFirstCoin immediately after CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered,

but the specification states that the machine becomes ready for a new order only once the beverage has been collected. The model therefore misses the collection step between drink completion and readiness.

Add an explicit post-preparation state and event for beverage collection in CoffeeMachine. After coffee or tea preparation completes, remain in a delivered or ready-to-collect state until a collection action occurs,
and only then transition back to WaitingForFirstCoin.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3
